title: clintonia udensis subsp. alpina
intro: Clintonia udensis subsp. alpina, also known as mountain fairy bells or alpine clintonia, is a rare, perennial plant species that belongs to the lily family (Liliaceae). The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects, making it useful in treating wounds, burns, and skin conditions. The health benefits of Clintonia udensis subsp. alpina include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and exhibiting antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
